{"name": "ecommerce-codebase-audit", "action": "edit", "content": "--- name: ecommerce-codebase-audit description: "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, auth cookie debugging, deployment follow-through, and post-deployment client readiness review." version: 1.6.0 tags: [nextjs, ecommerce, admin, audit, tailwind, layout, sidebar, supabase, auth, cloudflare, org-restructuring, package-extraction] --- # Ecommerce Codebase Audit Inspect and improve a Next.js e-commerce admin panel. Focus on layout consistency, theme application, page structure, image assets, and auth flow. ## Audit Checklist ### Layout Consistency - [ ] AdminShell lives in `app/admin/layout.tsx` (centralized layout), NOT in individual pages - [ ] Run `grep -rn "AdminShell" app/admin/ --include="page.tsx"` — should return zero results - [ ] `AdminShell` includes the sidebar navigation (responsive: hidden on mobile, fixed on desktop) - [ ] Sidebar uses the same links and active-state highlighting across all pages - [ ] All pages render inside `` via the root layout, not per-page wrappers ### Theme System The admin theme uses emerald/zinc on a dark background (#0a0a0b / #0f0f10): ```tsx // Tailwind classes to look for bg-[#0f0f10] // sidebar + card backgrounds bg-zinc-800/60 // borders text-emerald-400 // active text bg-emerald-500/10 // active background text-zinc-400 // inactive text hover:bg-zinc-800/40 // hover state ``` - [ ] All pages use the same color tokens (not hardcoded different colors) - [ ] Active page shows `bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-400` with indicator dot - [ ] Inactive pages use `text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-800/40` - [ ] All interactive elements have `transition-all` for smooth hover states - [ ] Borders use `zinc-800/60` or `zinc-700/60` — never crude `gray-200` or default tailwind borders ### Page Structure Each admin page follows this pattern. IMPORTANT: AdminShell is NOT imported by individual pages — it is applied by app/admin/layout.tsx: ```tsx 'use client' import { useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" export default function PageName() { const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null return (
{/* content — no AdminShell wrapper needed */}
) } ``` - [ ] Pages use `{ authed } = useAdminAuth()` for auth guard - [ ] Pages do NOT import AdminShell (layout handles it) - [ ] Pages use `fetch("/api/admin/*")` for data (not direct Supabase client) - [ ] Data fetched via `/api/admin/` routes that use service_role key server-side ### Admin UI Design Standards (El Viajero) When building or redesigning admin data table pages, follow these standards from the El Viajero productos page redesign: #### Table Column Widths Use fixed Tailwind width classes on `` elements so the table fills properly without huddling: | Column Type | Width | Notes | |------------|-------|-------| | Image/thumbnail | `w-12` | Small square, package icon placeholder when missing | | Name | fill | Remaining space, with brand as sub-label | | Price | `w-28` | Font-semibold, optionally show price_before as strikethrough | | Stock | `w-16` | Colored dot indicator (emerald >10, amber >0, red =0) | | Category | `w-28` | Pill badge style | | Actions | `w-40` | Right-aligned icon buttons | #### Action Buttons (NEVER text links) Replace text links like "Editar | Duplicar | Eliminar" with lucide icon buttons: | Action | Icon | Hover Color | Hover BG | |--------|------|-------------|----------| | Edit | `Pencil` | `text-blue-400` | `bg-blue-500/10` | | Duplicate | `Copy` | `text-amber-400` | `bg-amber-500/10` | | Delete | `Trash2` | `text-red-400` | `bg-red-500/10` | Each icon button: `rounded-lg p-1.5` with `opacity-70 group-hover:opacity-100` on the container for subtle reveal. Add `title` attribute for tooltip context. #### Search Bar - Place below the header row, prominent, with a `Search` lucide icon on the left - Max-width `max-w-md` so it doesn't over-extend - Include a clear button (`X` icon) when text is present - Use `rounded-xl` and `bg-zinc-900/50` with `border-zinc-800` - Focus ring: `focus:border-emerald-500/50 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-emerald-500/20` - Filter results locally by name, category, and brand #### Stock Indicator Show stock count with colored dot: ```tsx 10 ? "text-emerald-400" : stock > 0 ? "text-amber-400" : "text-red-400" }`}> 10 ? "bg-emerald-400" : stock > 0 ? "bg-amber-400" : "bg-red-400" }`} /> {stock} ``` #### Pagination Full pagination bar with: - Page range display: `"1-20 de 100 productos"` - Prev/Next buttons with `ChevronLeft`/`ChevronRight` icons, disabled state - Page number buttons with active state (`emerald` bg highlight) - Ellipsis (`...`) for gaps - Each page button: `w-7 h-7 rounded-lg text-xs font-medium` #### Empty State Use `Package` icon (or page-appropriate lucide icon) centered: ```tsx

{search ? "No results" : "No items yet"}

{!search && }
``` #### Toast Notifications Fixed top-right toast for success/error feedback: ```tsx
{type === "success" ? : } {text}
``` #### Image Thumbnails - Fixed size `h-9 w-9 rounded-lg border` - Clickable to open full-image modal - Fallback: `Package` lucide icon on `bg-zinc-800/50` when `image_url` is missing or is `.svg` - Active ring: `hover:ring-2 hover:ring-emerald-500/40` #### Inline Edit Mode When a row enters edit mode: - Replace display elements with `input` elements using `rounded-lg bg-zinc-800` - Use `focus:outline-none focus:border-emerald-500/50` on all inputs - Show Save/Cancel buttons replacing the action icons - Save button: `bg-emerald-600` (green); Cancel: `border-zinc-700/60` (outlined) ### AdminShell Component Located in `@/components/admin/admin-layout` (or wherever it is imported from). The AdminShell provides: - Sidebar navigation with 4 groups: Operaciones, Catálogo, Marketing, Configuración - Mobile responsiveness (hidden sidebar on mobile, toggle with hamburger) - Auth guard via `useAdminAuth` - Consistent background and layout padding The AdminShell is imported by `app/admin/layout.tsx` (the root admin layout) — NOT by individual pages. ### Data Table Loading States Always implement ALL three states: | State | Visual | Implementation | |-------|--------|---------------| | Loading | Skeleton rows with pulse animation | `animate-pulse` on placeholder divs matching cell shape | | Empty | Icon + text + optional CTA | Lucide icon + message + Create button | | Data | Full table with hover states | `group hover:bg-zinc-800/30` on each `` | For the loading skeleton, match the eventual cell content's shape and count: ```tsx {loading && Array.from({ length: 6 }).map((_, i) => (
))} ``` ### Search Bar Design (Detail) ```tsx
{ setSearch(e.target.value); setPage(1) }} placeholder="Search placeholder..." className="w-full rounded-xl border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/50 pl-9 pr-9 py-2.5 text-sm text-white placeholder-zinc-500 focus:outline-none focus:border-emerald-500/50 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-emerald-500/20 transition-all" /> {search && ( )}
``` ### Bulk Operations Panel When a page has bulk operations (e.g. price update), use a collapsible panel: ```tsx {showBulk && (

Title

)} ``` ### Auth Pattern ```tsx 'use client' import { useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null ``` Note: `AdminShell` is NOT imported here — it comes from `app/admin/layout.tsx`. Login stores session in localStorage + elviajero_admin_token cookie. useAdminAuth tries localStorage → Supabase SSR cookie → custom cookie, all via /api/auth with Authorization: Bearer header. ## Auth Flow & Cookie Debugging ### The Core Trap: Two Auth Paths Crossing In Next.js + Supabase e-commerce projects, there are usually TWO auth paths that can conflict: **Path A — Consumer auth (useAuth):** - Login page calls `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` directly from the browser client - Supabase's `@supabase/ssr` browser client sets the session cookie automatically - `/mi-cuenta` reads session via `supabase.auth.getSession()` — works because cookie exists **Path B — Admin auth (useAdminAuth / /api/auth):** - Login page calls `/api/auth` with `action: "login"` - API route creates an SSR client with `createServerClient()`, but often has `setAll() {}` empty - **The session cookie never gets set on the response** — the API returns tokens, but no cookie - Admin layout falls back to localStorage or custom cookie **Common failure modes:** 1. **Login works (gets tokens) but /mi-cuenta shows unauthenticated** — consumer login page fetches `/api/auth` instead of calling Supabase directly. Fix: use `useAuth().login()` (Supabase browser client) which sets the correct session cookie. 2. **Login works but /admin redirects back to /login** — admin layout's `useAdminAuth()` calls `/api/auth?action=me` with a token from localStorage, but the API route's token verification path fails. Fix: ensure the API route can verify tokens against Supabase REST API (not just SSR cookies). ### Fix Checklist - [ ] Consumer login uses Supabase browser client directly (`supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`), NOT a custom `/api/auth` endpoint - [ ] After successful login, store session tokens in `localStorage` for admin layout fallback: `localStorage.setItem("elviajero_admin_session", JSON.stringify({ access_token, refresh_token }))` - [ ] Admin layout checks localStorage first, then Supabase SSR cookie, then custom cookie — triple fallback - [ ] API route `action: "me"` verifies tokens via `fetch(${supabaseUrl}/auth/v1/user)` not `supabase.auth.getUser()` - [ ] Service-role client for profile queries uses `createClient(url, serviceRoleKey)` — passing service key as second arg, not in headers - [ ] Role check queries DB profiles table (not JWT claim) — `profiles.role` not `user.role` ### Debugging Auth Issues ```bash # Check if API route is reachable curl -sX POST https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/api/auth \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"action":"login","email":"[REDACTED_EMAIL]","password":"test"}' | head -c 200 # Check if session cookie is set curl -sv https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/mi-cuenta 2>&1 | grep -i 'set-cookie' # Check server logs docker service logs elviajero-comercio_web --tail 30 --no-task-ids ``` ## Visual Asset Workflow ### Full Asset Overhaul Pattern When doing a complete visual assets redesign (inventory, generation, deployment): #### Phase 1: Inventory ```bash # List all files on disk find public/images -type f | sort # List all code references grep -rohP '"/images/[^"'"'"']+' --include='*.{tsx,ts,json,css}' | sed 's/^"//' | sort -u # Cross-reference: 3 outcomes per image # 1. Referenced + on disk = OK # 2. Referenced + missing = FIX (add file) # 3. On disk + no reference = ORPHAN (delete) # Size audit — small = programmatic (low quality) find public/images -name "*.png" -size -50k ``` #### Phase 2: Categorization | Type | Size threshold | Keep as | Upgrade? | |------|---------------|---------|----------| | SVGs | varies | Vector | Only if they're emoji text | | Real photos | >50KB | PNG | No | | Programmatic renders | <50KB | PNG | Yes — regenerate with richer Pillow composites | | Icons | 4-8KB | PNG | Intentionally small — minimalist silhouettes | | Hero backgrounds | <20KB | PNG | Yes — add atmosphere, silhouettes, gradients | #### Phase 3: Generation (no AI API available) Use Pillow for progressive enhancement rounds: **Round 1 — Placeholders:** ```python # Sky gradient + bokeh circles + category silhouettes # Write to a generate script, deploy, iterate ``` **Round 2 — Richer details:** - Tree silhouettes, stars, glowing tent, moon - River reflections, fishing rod, sun at horizon - Mountain trails, hiker with backpack - Headlight beams, road lines, car approaching - Dust clouds, motorcycle, fence - Fields, barn, sun, clouds **Round 3 — AI (if REPLICATE_API_TOKEN or equivalent available):** ```python if call_replicate(prompt, w, h, path): print("AI generated") else: render_fallback(path) # keep best Pillow version ``` **Round 4 — Self-critique:** After deploying, review each asset and write a brutal critique doc. Then regenerate the worst offenders in the next round. #### Phase 4: Deployment Pitfalls **Cloudflare cache (IMPORTANT):** - CF sets `cache-control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` — 1 YEAR cache - No CF API key? Only option is **rename the file** (logo.png → logo-v2.png) - Then update ALL code references to point to new filename - Verify with curl, not browser: `curl -sk URL/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B"` - Then tell the user to hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) **Docker layer cache:** - If files exist locally but return 404 on deploy, the Docker COPY was cached - Fix: `docker build --no-cache` or touch a file in the COPY layer **Verify the right file is served:** ```bash # Check HTML references curl -sk https://example.com/ | grep -oP '(logo|favicon)-v2' # Check file directly curl -sk https://example.com/images/logo-v2.png -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}B" ``` ### Key Project-Specific Patterns (El Viajero) - Product images: `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/products/*.png` (34 products) - Category backgrounds (dynamic SVG): `/images/categories/${slug}.svg` — slug values in `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` - Category heroes: heroImage prop → `app/categoria/*/page.tsx` → `/images/marketing/hero-*.png` - Hero carousel: content-driven array in `content/es.json` → `/images/marketing/hero-carousel-*.png` - Testimonials: `"image"` field in `content/es.json` → `/images/testimonials/avatar-*.png` - Logo: `header.tsx` → `/images/logo.png` (or `logo-v2.png` after cache-bust) - Favicon: `layout.tsx` → `/images/favicon.png` (or `favicon-v2.png`) - OG image: `layout.tsx` → `/images/og-viajero.svg` - PWA icons: `manifest.json` → `/images/icon-192.png`, `/images/icon-512.png` ### Generation Recipes **Category icons (128x128):** Create a Python script with `ImageDraw` — tent, fish, umbrella, backpack, car, motorcycle, barn silhouettes on green circle background. These are intentionally small (4-8KB). **Hero banners (1920x600):** Create scene composites: 1. Sky gradient (top to bottom, warm/cool depending on scene) 2. Ground layer (dark rectangle) 3. Scene elements (tree silhouettes, stars, moon, tent, river, fishing rod, mountains, road, headlights, barn, fence, sun) 4. Bokeh overlay (random circles with low alpha) 5. Gaussian blur (radius 0.3-0.5) for polish **Testimonial avatars (200x200):** If no AI available, create colored circles with basic face features (eyes, smile, hair shape, shirt). Tell the client these are placeholders for real customer photos. **PWA icons:** Render from the logo at 192x192 and 512x512 with anti-aliasing. ## Multi-Locale Content Updates (PAB Sites) When updating content across all locales (en/es/nl/de) in a PAB (Paragu-AI Builder) project: ### Safe approach vs. pitfalls 1. **Python JSON manipulation is safer than `patch` tool for large content changes.** The `patch` tool works well for small string replacements, but when replacing JSON blocks spanning 50+ lines, it can match the wrong text, leave orphaned content, and produce valid-but-wrong JSON. This happened when replacing the `processPage` section — patch matched a subset, leaving trailing orphan that was syntactically valid but structurally wrong. 2. **When `patch` goes wrong: stop, don't stack.** If the first patch produces unexpected results (orphan content, duplicated sections, lost keys), do NOT apply more patches on top. Switch immediately to Python `json.load()/json.dump()` — read the broken file, fix the data structure in memory, and write it back clean. Stacking patches compounds the mistakes and makes the file increasingly fragile to further edits. The `processPage` fix required three patches to clean up what one wrong match did. 3. **`patch` can match content from a previously-applied patch** — If a file was already modified, the old_string might not match what's actually in the file. Always read the file fresh before patching, and verify with a direct key inspection after (`python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('f')); print(d.get('targetKey',{}).keys())"`). 4. **Shell variable expansion fails in `execute_code`'s multi-line Python strings** — `for f in $FILES; do python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('$f'))"; done` breaks because `$f` isn't expanded inside the Python string. Use explicit file paths, a temp script, or Python's own `glob` module instead. 5. **Always validate JSON structure after content changes, not just syntax.** A file can be valid JSON but structurally wrong (missing keys, wrong step counts, orphan content). After validation: ```bash python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('file'))" # syntax check ``` Also verify structural integrity: ```python d["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] # should have 5 steps d["home"]["services"]["groups"] # should have 3 groups d["aboutPage"]["story"]["paragraphs"] # should have 3 paragraphs ``` 6. **When deleting orphaned content from a botched patch, read the file with JSON parser first**, then find the structural key that contains the orphan data. Delete it from the Python dict and re-write. Don't try to surgically remove lines with patch. 7. **Process step consistency rule:** The same process narrative lives in 6 places across the site. Update ALL of these together in the same script block — missing any one creates inconsistency: - `home.process.steps` (homepage) - `processPage.process.steps` (process page) - `landingInversor.process.steps`, `landingTrust.process.steps`, `landingLifestyle.process.steps`, `landingEmpresa.process.steps` (LinkedIn ad landing pages) 8. **Process for adding new content sections across locales:** - Step 1: Define the content data structure in a Python dict with all localizations - Step 2: Load each locale file, apply transformations, write back - Step 3: Validate JSON syntax for all files - Step 4: Verify structural keys match expectations (count groups, steps, paragraphs) ```python for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]: with open(f"content/{lang}.json") as f: d = json.load(f) d["parentSection"]["newKey"] = translations[lang] with open(f"content/{lang}.json", "w") as f: json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) ``` 9. **Section structure for the 'Our Services' pattern:** ```python data["home"]["services"] = { "eyebrow": "Our Services", "title": "Section title", "groups": [ {"id": "group-id", "title": "Group Name", "subtitle": "Group subtitle.", "items": [{"title": "Service", "description": "Desc.", "icon": "IconName"}]} ] } ``` 10. **Section structure for the 'Story' pattern:** ```python data["aboutPage"]["story"] = { "title": "Our Story", "eyebrow": "Tagline", "paragraphs": ["Para 1.", "Para 2.", "Para 3."] } ``` 11. **Section structure for the 'Process steps' pattern:** ```python data["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] = [ {"number": 1, "title": "Step Name", "description": "Long description.", "icon": "IconName", "image": {"$img": "process.key"}, "duration": "Week 1"} ] ``` 12. **Landing page Calendly URL pattern:** ```python base = "https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/consulta" utm = f"utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign={campaign}" url = f"{base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-{placement}-{lang}" ``` ## Deployment ### Docker Deployment After modifying content assets (images, content JSON, or static files): 1. **Rebuild** — `npm run build` catches missing images and type errors 2. **Rebuild Docker image** — `docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .` 3. **Deploy** — `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false` 4. **Verify** — `curl -sI https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/ | head -5` 5. **Force rolling update** — if same image tag, `docker service update --force elviajero-comercio_web` Never assume changes to static content or images are live — Docker rebuild and stack deploy are required. Browser cache is NOT the server — always curl first. ### Production Monitoring After every deploy, add: - Healthcheck endpoint at `/api/health` returning `{status, timestamp, uptime}` - Docker HEALTHCHECK in Dockerfile (every 30s, 40s start grace, 3 retries) - Cron healthcheck via Hermes: every 15m, auto-redeploy on failure - Shell check script: `/root/scripts/check-{project}.sh` ### Docker Build Timeout `docker build` with npm install can take >120s on cold cache. Set terminal timeout to 300s for builds. If it times out, retry — the npm cache is warm on the second run and it will complete faster. #### Next.js `pages/` Folder Conflict (v16+) Next.js 16+ errors with "`pages` and `app` directories should be under the same folder" when a `pages/` data directory exists at project root alongside `src/app/`. Fix: 1. Rename `pages/` to `nexa-pages/` 2. Update all `page.tsx` references from `'pages'` to `'nexa-pages'` 3. Update Dockerfile to copy `nexa-pages/` into the standalone output 4. Run `docker build --no-cache` after rename (Docker layer cache may use old path) #### Docker Standalone + Data Files Next.js `output: "standalone"` produces a `server.js` that does `process.chdir(__dirname)` — so `process.cwd()` points to `.next/standalone/`, not the app root. Data files read at runtime via `readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), ...))` must be copied into `.next/standalone/`: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./ COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./.next/standalone/nexa-pages COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./.next/standalone/content ``` Without this, the container runs fine but returns static HTML with fallback data because `readFileSync` silently fails and the catch block returns defaults. #### The "Welcome" Bug (Static SSG Fallback) When a PAB page always renders "Welcome" regardless of content changes, the root cause is in the page component's fallback chain: ```tsx // BROKEN: home.json has 'titleKey' not 'title' — always falls through {homePage.title || content?.title || 'Welcome'} // FIXED: use siteName from the locale JSON {content?.siteName || 'Nexa Paraguay'} ``` Check that `getPages()` and `getContent()` actually read files via `readFileSync`/`readdirSync` at build time. The `catch` block silently swallowing the error is the symptom — verify the files are accessible during the Docker build stage. #### `window.gtag` Type Conflicts When a project uses `declare global { interface Window { gtag?: any } }` and imports from `@ai-whisperers/seo/analytics` (which also declares it), you get: ``` Type error: All declarations of 'gtag' must have identical modifiers. ``` **Fix:** Use `(window as any).gtag` everywhere instead of declaring `interface Window`: ```typescript const w = window as any w.gtag("event", "page_view", data) ``` Apply to all GA4 tracking files: `components/analytics.tsx`, `lib/analytics.ts`. ## Traefik + Docker Swarm Routing Setup When deploying a new Next.js client site to an existing Docker Swarm + Traefik infrastructure (paragu-ai.com VPS at 72.61.44.159): ### 1. DNS Record (Cloudflare) Add an A record in the Cloudflare dashboard for the paragu-ai.com zone: ``` Type: A Name: {site-name} Content: 72.61.44.159 Proxy status: Proxied (orange cloud) ``` ### 2. docker-compose.yml (Traefik Labels) The template's `docker-compose.yml` has superspuma's labels. Update them: ```yaml services: web: build: . image: {site-name}:prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 resources: limits: { memory: 512M, cpus: '0.5' } reservations: { memory: 128M, cpus: '0.1' } labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.rule=Host(`{site-name}.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers.{site-name}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services.{site-name}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" networks: agent-net: external: true ``` Pitfall: the `image` field in docker-compose.yml must match the `docker build -t` tag. ### 3. Deploy (Manual on VPS) ```bash ssh root@72.61.44.159 cd /root/{repo-name} git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) # for @ai-whisperers npm packages npm ci --legacy-peer-deps npm run build docker build -t {site-name}:prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name} ``` After deploy, verify: ```bash curl -sI https://{site-name}.paragu-ai.com/ | grep "HTTP/" ``` ### 4. Docker Build GITHUB_TOKEN Issue The standard Docker multi-stage build (template's Dockerfile) runs `npm install` inside the build container but doesn't have access to the GITHUB_TOKEN for @ai-whisperers GitHub Packages registry. **Two solutions:** **Option A (simple):** Build on the host, then use a production-only Dockerfile that copies the already-built output: ```dockerfile # Dockerfile.prod — used after `npm run build` on host FROM node:20-alpine AS runner WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0" RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs COPY --chown=nextjs:nodejs . . USER nextjs EXPOSE 3000 CMD ["node_modules/.bin/next", "start"] ``` ```bash # On VPS: npm run build # host build has GITHUB_TOKEN docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t site:prod . # just ships node_modules + .next docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml site ``` **Option B (Docker build-arg):** Pass the token as a build arg and set it in the .npmrc at build time: ```dockerfile FROM node:20-alpine AS deps WORKDIR /app COPY package.json package-lock.json* .npmrc ./ ARG NODE_AUTH_TOKEN RUN echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}" >> .npmrc && \ npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` ```bash docker build --build-arg NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) -t site:prod . ``` **Pitfall:** Docker BuildKit blocks ARG values from ENV for security ("Do not use ARG or ENV instructions for sensitive data"). Option A is more reliable. ### 5. CI/CD Workflow The reusable ci-cd workflows (Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd) may not be accessible from new repos due to permissions. Example standalone deploy.yml as fallback: ```yaml name: Deploy on: push: branches: [main] jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20' cache: 'npm' - run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps - run: npm run build - name: Setup SSH run: | mkdir -p ~/.ssh echo "${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ssh-keyscan -H ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true - name: Deploy via SSH run: | ssh ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}@${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }} \ "cd /root/{repo-name} && git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main && \ export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) && npm ci --legacy-peer-deps && npm run build && \ docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t {site-name}:prod . && \ docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {site-name}" ``` ## Google Maps Browser Navigation for Search-Engine-Blocked Research When search engines (Google, DuckDuckGo, Bing, Brave) block automated queries with CAPTCHAs/IP blocks, use browser_navigate to Google Maps as a workaround: ### Pattern 1. Navigate to Google Maps search URL directly: ```bash https://www.google.com/maps/search/{query}+{location} ``` 2. The browser snapshot shows results as readable `article` elements with: - Company name (clickable) - Rating (stars) - Address - Phone number - Hours - Website link (when available) 3. Click a result to view more details in the side panel. 4. To refine, clear the combobox and type a new query, then press Enter. ### Queries That Work Spanish-language queries work better for Paraguayan businesses: ``` "constructora civil Asuncion Paraguay" "vidrio templado laminado fachada Asuncion" "fachada vidrio aluminio Asuncion Paraguay" "desarrolladora inmobiliaria Asuncion Paraguay" "arquitecto Asuncion Paraguay fachada" ``` ### Limitations - Only shows ~7 results per view (Google Maps limited view without login) - No API access to extract full data programmatically - Phone numbers and emails must be copied manually from snapshots - Results are biased toward Google's ranking, not comprehensive - Works for Paraguay because Google Maps has good coverage there ## B2B Lead-Gen Conversion (e-commerce template → B2B site) When converting the e-commerce template (template-nextjs-client) into a B2B lead-generation site (construction materials, industrial supply, wholesale), follow this pattern: ### What to REMOVE from the template ``` B2C pages to delete: app/tienda/ → product store with cart/checkout app/envio/ → shipping info app/promociones/ → promotions app/producto/[slug]/ → individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) B2C components to delete: search-autocomplete.tsx → no product search for B2B dark-mode-toggle.tsx → B2B stays light theme payment-methods.tsx → no payments on lead-gen site quick-order.tsx → no quick ordering product-card.tsx → replace with spec-card pattern hero-carousel.tsx → use static hero for B2B promo-carousel.tsx → no promos bottom-nav.tsx → not needed for B2B mobile empty-state.tsx → no shopping experience recently-viewed.tsx → no browsing history wishlist.ts / lib/ → no wishlists ``` ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Post-Deployment Content Audit / Roast Pattern After deploying a converted B2B site (or any new client site from template), run a systematic content audit by browsing every page live and checking for: 1. **Template remnants**: Any text/images from the original template site that leaked through (Fun4Me brand, superspuma references, wrong Instagram handles). Check /contacto, /nosotros, /faq, footer especially — these are the most commonly overlooked. 2. **Content that doesn't match the business type**: - B2B construction site still showing e-commerce order tracking - Professional services site saying "bienestar íntimo" (sex shop) - Law firm showing shipping/returns information 3. **Placeholder phone numbers**: Any hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` (Fun4Me's number) that wasn't replaced. 4. **Broken links**: Links pointing to removed pages (/tienda, /envio, /producto/[slug]). 5. **Empty portfolio/projects**: "Coming soon" or "próximamente" on a B2B site that needs trust signals. Replace with a CTA-driven alternative. ### Content Audit / Roast Methodology Browser every page on the live site using browser_navigate and check: | Page | Common Issues | |------|---------------| | /contacto | Fun4Me Instagram, returns/orders/shipping links, wrong phone | | /nosotros | "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, sex shop story, purple heart, /tienda links | | /faq | Generic questions instead of industry-specific technical answers | | /proyectos | "Coming soon" instead of real portfolio | | /productos | Emoji icons instead of real product images | | /blog | No images, missing headers, broken formatting | After audit, fix the 3 worst pages (usually contacto, nosotros, faq) in one commit, then re-deploy and verify. ### Full list of Fun4Me artifacts to hunt when converting template When converting the template to a B2B site, these Fun4Me-specific traces survive even after page/component deletion: | File | What to fix | |------|-------------| | `app/contacto/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"`, Instagram `@fun4me_py`, "Estado del pedido" / "Reclamo / devolución" quick links | | `app/nosotros/page.tsx` | `brand.story` fallback to "bienestar íntimo con total privacidad" (sex shop), "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section, purple heart emoji, `/tienda` link | | `app/sitemap.ts` | Hardcoded `base = "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com"`, fun4me page paths | | `content/es.json` | `analytics.ga4` empty string (needs real GA4 ID), `home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref: "/tienda"` | | `layout.tsx` | Theme-color `#1B5E20` (green, not blue B2B), metadata title fallbacks | | `components/` | Any file still importing `@/components/search-autocomplete` or `dark-mode-toggle` | | `docker-compose.yml` | Traefik labels referencing old site name (`superspuma`) | | Blog post `[slug]/page.tsx` | Hardcoded `WA_PHONE = "[REDACTED_PHONE]"` | After content/es.json fixes, check the about/nosotros pages still reference `brand` keys (which were Fun4Me-specific) instead of `nosotros` keys (which have the B2B content). Fix by rewriting page.tsx to use the correct content section. | Page | Purpose | Content Pattern | |------|---------|-----------------| | /productos | Product catalog with specs | Technical data + "request quote" CTA (no prices) | | /proyectos | Portfolio gallery | Image + building name + scope + year | | /servicios | Service listing | 5-6 service cards with icons | | /cotizar | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product type (multi-select), qty, timeline → WhatsApp | ### Content Model (es.json) Home page sections for B2B: 1. **Hero**: gradient background, headline focusing on B2B value prop, two CTAs (quote form + WhatsApp) 2. **Features grid**: 4-column cards (stock local, certified quality, tech support, payment terms) 3. **Product preview**: 4 cards linking to /productos anchors 4. **Testimonials**: 2-3 B2B client quotes 5. **Final CTA**: Dark gradient, WhatsApp button Product catalog structure: ```json { "products": [ { "id": "product-slug", "name": "Product Name", "description": "Technical description for B2B buyers", "features": ["Feature 1", "Feature 2"], "applications": ["Building type 1", "Building type 2"], "cta": "Request quote for X" } ] } ``` Quote form on /cotizar: collects name, company, email, phone, project type (dropdown), product type (multi-select buttons), approximate qty/m2, timeline, message. On submit → opens WhatsApp with pre-formatted message. ### Theme Use a LIGHT theme for construction/B2B (not dark): ```json { "primary": "#0F62FE", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA" } ``` Remove dark-mode-toggle. The template already supports light mode via content/tokens.json `"theme": "light"`. ### Header/Footer Adaptation Header: replace Fun4Me brand with client name. Replace nav items. Footer: replace brand name, update columns for B2B context. ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Debugging When the template has `@ai-whisperers/*` dependencies with `^0.1.0` ranges that don't match the published registry: 1. Check actual latest versions: ```bash for pkg in @ai-whisperers/ui @ai-whisperers/admin @ai-whisperers/seo; do echo -n "$pkg: "; npm view $pkg version 2>&1 done ``` 2. Published versions differ per package: | Package | Latest | |---------|--------| | @ai-whisperers/ui | 0.4.0 | | @ai-whisperers/admin | 0.2.1 | | @ai-whisperers/seo | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/theme | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/auth | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/commerce | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/whatsapp | 0.2.0 | | @ai-whisperers/i18n | 0.2.0 | 3. Set each to its correct version (NOT all to same). The `^0.1.0` range in the template was never updated after the packages reached 0.2.x/0.4.x. 4. Some packages (like `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest`) don't exist — remove from devDependencies. 5. @ai-whisperers/seo 0.2.0 has an empty dist directory — the AnalyticsProvider import fails. Workaround: remove the AnalyticsProvider from layout.tsx and add GA4 via plain `
